disclaimer: ALL content in this file is generated by AI for audit preparation assistance only. It has NO regulatory authority. It must NEVER be presented to auditors as regulatory text or official ECC requirements. Official compliance determinations must reference Layer 1 verbatim text from ECC-2:2024.

audit_questions: Can the organization produce a current, board-approved cybersecurity strategy document?
Does the strategy reference national cybersecurity objectives (NCA and applicable sector regulators)?
Is the strategy approved by the head of the entity or their formally designated delegate (Authorized Official)?
Are the strategy goals demonstrably aligned with relevant legislative and regulatory requirements?
suggested_evidence: Signed cybersecurity strategy document with approval date and Authorized Official's signature
Board or executive meeting minutes referencing strategy approval
Traceability matrix linking strategy goals to NCA requirements and applicable sector regulations
indicators_of_implementation: Strategy document exists, is dated within the current review cycle, and carries the Authorized Official's approval
Strategy goals are traceable to specific legislative and regulatory requirements
Cybersecurity objectives in the strategy are reflected in operational programs and budget allocations

maturity_signals.initial: Strategy exists as a document but has never been formally approved by the Authorized Official or is materially outdated
maturity_signals.developing: Strategy is approved and communicated; goals are loosely linked to regulatory requirements
maturity_signals.defined: Strategy drives budget and KPIs; goals are explicitly mapped to NCA and sector regulatory requirements
maturity_signals.advanced: Strategy is integrated into enterprise risk management and is benchmarked and updated in response to changes in national frameworks

possible_documents: Board / Executive Resolution or Approval Record

audit_questions: Is there a formally documented action plan derived from the cybersecurity strategy?
Does the action plan include owners, timelines, and measurable milestones?
Is there evidence that the action plan is actively executed (progress tracking, status updates)?
Are action plan items tied back to specific strategy objectives?
suggested_evidence: Cybersecurity action plan or roadmap document with assigned owners and target dates
Progress reports or project status dashboards showing execution against the plan
Meeting minutes or steering committee records referencing plan progress
indicators_of_implementation: An action plan exists that is directly traceable to strategy objectives
Each initiative has an assigned responsible party and timeline
Regular progress reviews occur and results are documented

maturity_signals.initial: Action plan exists on paper but has no assigned owners or progress tracking
maturity_signals.developing: Plan is tracked periodically; some milestones have been met
maturity_signals.defined: Plan is integrated into project governance; progress is formally reported to the cybersecurity supervisory committee
maturity_signals.advanced: Plan adapts dynamically to risk landscape changes and is reviewed at each governance cycle

possible_technical_evidence: Project management tool export (e.g., JIRA, Microsoft Project, Planner) showing cybersecurity initiatives

audit_questions: Is there a defined review schedule for the cybersecurity strategy (e.g., annual)?
Is there evidence of the most recent strategy review, including its date and outcomes?
Is the review triggered not only on schedule but also upon changes to relevant legislative or regulatory requirements?
Are review results documented and approved by the Authorized Official?
suggested_evidence: Strategy review log or version history showing review dates and approvers
Updated strategy document with change history
Communication records showing notification of regulatory changes that triggered an unscheduled review
indicators_of_implementation: A review schedule is defined and documented in the strategy itself or a governing procedure
The most recent review date falls within the defined cycle
Any material regulatory changes since last review have been assessed against the strategy

maturity_signals.initial: No defined review cycle; strategy has not been reviewed since initial approval
maturity_signals.developing: Reviews occur but are ad-hoc; not triggered consistently by regulatory changes
maturity_signals.defined: Reviews are conducted on schedule and upon regulatory changes; outcomes are documented
maturity_signals.advanced: Review process is integrated with regulatory intelligence feed; changes are assessed within a defined SLA

audit_questions: Is there a formally established cybersecurity department within the entity?
Is the cybersecurity department structurally independent from the IT and Communications Department?
Is the independence requirement traceable to High Order No. 37140 (dated 14/08/1438H)?
Does the cybersecurity department's reporting line go to the head of the entity or their delegate without creating a conflict of interest?
suggested_evidence: Approved organizational chart showing the cybersecurity department as a distinct unit separate from IT
Founding document, executive order, or board resolution establishing the cybersecurity department
Documentation of the reporting line to the Authorized Official or equivalent
indicators_of_implementation: An independent cybersecurity department exists in the official organizational structure
The department is not subordinate to or merged with IT/Communications
Reporting line to the head of the entity or their delegate is formally documented

maturity_signals.initial: Cybersecurity functions exist within IT but no independent department has been established
maturity_signals.developing: A separate cybersecurity unit exists but reporting line independence is informal
maturity_signals.defined: Department is formally established, independent, and reporting structure is documented and approved
maturity_signals.advanced: Cybersecurity department has a seat at the executive table; independence is audited periodically and conflicts of interest are formally assessed

possible_technical_evidence: HR system org chart export showing department hierarchy

audit_questions: Are all cybersecurity positions (not just head and critical roles) filled with full-time Saudi nationals?
Are all cybersecurity staff demonstrably qualified (certifications, degrees, or documented competencies)?
Is there a process to verify nationality and qualifications at hiring and periodically thereafter?
Are vacancies in cybersecurity positions documented with a plan to fill them with qualifying candidates?
suggested_evidence: HR records or staffing roster for all cybersecurity positions showing nationality and employment type (full-time)
Qualification records: academic certificates, professional certifications (e.g., CISSP, CISM, CEH)
Hiring policy or procedure requiring Saudi nationality and qualifications for all cybersecurity roles
indicators_of_implementation: 100% of filled cybersecurity positions are held by full-time Saudi nationals
Each incumbent can demonstrate professional qualification relevant to their role
HR onboarding procedure includes nationality verification and qualification check for cybersecurity positions

maturity_signals.initial: Only head and critical positions comply; other cybersecurity roles filled with non-Saudis or part-time staff
maturity_signals.developing: Majority of positions comply; gaps are documented with remediation plans
maturity_signals.defined: All positions comply; compliance is verified at hire and reviewed periodically
maturity_signals.advanced: Competency framework exists for all cybersecurity roles; qualification compliance is reported to the supervisory committee

possible_documents: Cybersecurity Staff Roster with Nationality and Employment Type
HR Hiring Policy for Cybersecurity Positions
possible_technical_evidence: HR system export filtered on cybersecurity department showing citizenship and employment status

audit_questions: Has a cybersecurity supervisory committee been formally established by the Authorized Official?
Are the committee's members, responsibilities, and governance framework documented and approved?
Is the head of the cybersecurity department included as a mandatory committee member?
Does the committee's reporting line go to the head of the entity or their delegate without a conflict of interest?
suggested_evidence: Supervisory committee charter or terms of reference signed by the Authorized Official
Committee membership list showing the cybersecurity department head and other members
Committee meeting minutes demonstrating active oversight of cybersecurity programs
indicators_of_implementation: A committee charter exists and is approved by the Authorized Official
The cybersecurity department head is documented as a member
The committee meets at the defined frequency and minutes are recorded

maturity_signals.initial: No supervisory committee exists; oversight is informal
maturity_signals.developing: Committee exists but lacks a formal charter or meets irregularly
maturity_signals.defined: Committee is formally constituted, meets regularly, and produces documented outputs
maturity_signals.advanced: Committee reviews KPIs, receives audit findings, and escalates to the Authorized Official on a defined schedule

possible_documents: Cybersecurity Supervisory Committee Charter / Terms of Reference

audit_questions: Are cybersecurity policies and procedures formally identified, documented, and approved by the Authorized Official?
Do the policies cover all relevant cybersecurity controls and requirements?
Have the policies been communicated to all relevant personnel and internal parties?
Is there evidence of communication (e.g., distribution records, acknowledgment logs)?
suggested_evidence: Cybersecurity policy document suite with Authorized Official approval signatures and dates
Policy distribution records or communication logs (email, intranet, LMS)
Employee acknowledgment records
indicators_of_implementation: A complete set of cybersecurity policies exists, each with an approval date and version number
Policies have been formally communicated to all relevant staff
Acknowledgment or receipt of policies is recorded for all required personnel

maturity_signals.initial: Some policies exist but coverage is incomplete; formal approval is absent
maturity_signals.developing: Policies are approved but distribution is informal and not fully tracked
maturity_signals.defined: Complete policy set is approved, distributed, and acknowledgment is tracked
maturity_signals.advanced: Policy suite is mapped to technical controls with automated compliance testing; gaps trigger immediate remediation

possible_technical_evidence: LMS policy acknowledgment export
Intranet policy portal access logs

audit_questions: Is there evidence that cybersecurity policies and procedures are actually implemented, not just documented?
Are implementation results tracked and reported to relevant governance bodies?
Is there a mechanism to detect and address non-compliance with policies?
Are spot checks, audits, or compliance assessments conducted to verify implementation?
suggested_evidence: Compliance monitoring reports showing policy implementation status across departments
Audit or assessment reports verifying policy adherence
Non-compliance incident register with remediation actions
indicators_of_implementation: Implementation is monitored beyond documentation — there is evidence of operational adherence
Non-compliance cases are tracked and escalated appropriately
Department heads or control owners attest to implementation on a defined schedule

maturity_signals.initial: Policies are published but implementation is not verified
maturity_signals.developing: Periodic checks occur but results are not formally reported
maturity_signals.defined: Implementation is monitored systematically; results are reported to the supervisory committee
maturity_signals.advanced: Automated compliance monitoring with near-real-time dashboards; exception-based escalation is in place

possible_technical_evidence: SIEM or GRC platform compliance dashboard screenshots
Automated policy compliance scan results

audit_questions: Are cybersecurity policies and procedures supported by documented technical security standards?
Do technical standards exist for key components (firewalls, databases, operating systems, etc.)?
Are the technical standards aligned with and referenced by the corresponding policies?
Are the standards kept current with vendor guidance and industry benchmarks (e.g., CIS Benchmarks)?
suggested_evidence: Technical security standards library with reference to parent policies
Hardening or configuration standards for each major technology platform
Version history or review log demonstrating standards are kept current
indicators_of_implementation: Each key technology platform has a corresponding technical security standard
Standards are explicitly referenced in or linked to parent policies
Standards are reviewed and updated when technology platforms change or vendor guidance is updated

maturity_signals.initial: Technical standards exist for some platforms but are informal or not linked to policies
maturity_signals.developing: Standards library exists but coverage is incomplete; not all platforms have standards
maturity_signals.defined: Comprehensive standards library exists, is linked to policies, and has a defined review cycle
maturity_signals.advanced: Standards are version-controlled, mapped to CIS or equivalent benchmarks, and compliance is automatically tested

possible_technical_evidence: Configuration management database (CMDB) entries showing baseline standards
Automated hardening scan results compared against defined baselines

audit_questions: Is there a defined review schedule for cybersecurity policies and procedures?
Is the review triggered by regulatory or legislative changes in addition to scheduled intervals?
Are changes to policies documented with version history, change rationale, and new approval?
Is there evidence that the most recent review occurred within the defined cycle?
suggested_evidence: Policy review log showing all policies, last review date, next review date, and reviewer
Version-controlled policy documents with change history and approval signatures
Regulatory change register showing triggered policy reviews
indicators_of_implementation: All policies have a defined review interval documented within them
No policy has exceeded its review cycle without documented justification
Changes are tracked in version control and re-approved by the Authorized Official

maturity_signals.initial: Policies are not reviewed after initial approval; no version control exists
maturity_signals.developing: Some policies are reviewed periodically but not all; not consistently triggered by regulatory changes
maturity_signals.defined: All policies are in a review cycle; changes are documented and approved; regulatory triggers are monitored
maturity_signals.advanced: Automated policy management system tracks review dates and sends alerts; regulatory intelligence integration triggers reviews

possible_documents: Policy Review Log / Master Policy Register
possible_technical_evidence: Policy management system showing due dates and review completion status

audit_questions: Has the Authorized Official formally identified, documented, and approved the cybersecurity governance structure?
Are roles and responsibilities for cybersecurity clearly defined for all relevant parties?
Are persons assigned to each cybersecurity role formally designated?
Is there a mechanism to ensure these assignments do not create conflicts of interest?
Is the necessary support (resources, authority) formally documented as provided?
suggested_evidence: Approved cybersecurity governance framework or RACI matrix
Organizational chart with named cybersecurity role assignments
Formal appointment letters or role designation documents signed by the Authorized Official
Conflict-of-interest declaration records for key cybersecurity roles
indicators_of_implementation: A governance structure document exists with Authorized Official approval
All key cybersecurity roles are assigned to named individuals
Roles are distinct and do not create conflict-of-interest situations
Role assignments are reflected in job descriptions and HR records

maturity_signals.initial: Cybersecurity responsibilities are informally distributed with no documented governance structure
maturity_signals.developing: Governance structure exists but is not formally approved; some roles are ambiguous
maturity_signals.defined: Complete governance structure is formally approved; all roles are assigned and conflict-of-interest is assessed
maturity_signals.advanced: Governance structure is reviewed with each organizational change; effectiveness of role assignments is periodically assessed

possible_technical_evidence: HR system export showing role assignments for cybersecurity positions

audit_questions: Is there a defined schedule for reviewing cybersecurity roles and responsibilities?
Is the review triggered by organizational changes or regulatory updates, not only by scheduled intervals?
Are reviewed and updated role assignments formally approved?
Is there evidence of the most recent review date?
suggested_evidence: Roles and responsibilities review log with dates and approvers
Updated RACI matrix with version history
Change records triggered by organizational restructuring or regulatory updates
indicators_of_implementation: A review cycle is defined for roles and responsibilities
The most recent review is within the defined cycle
Reviews are triggered by organizational or regulatory changes between scheduled intervals

maturity_signals.initial: Roles are defined once and never reviewed; no record of updates
maturity_signals.developing: Reviews occur but are ad-hoc; not consistently triggered by change events
maturity_signals.defined: Reviews are on a defined schedule and triggered by changes; all updates are approved
maturity_signals.advanced: Role governance is integrated with HR change management; any org chart change automatically triggers a cybersecurity roles review

possible_documents: Revised RACI Matrix with Change History

audit_questions: Is there a formally documented and approved cybersecurity risk management methodology?
Does the methodology address confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information and technology assets?
Is the methodology approved by the Authorized Official or cybersecurity department head?
Does the methodology include risk identification, assessment, treatment, and monitoring procedures?
suggested_evidence: Cybersecurity risk management policy or methodology document with approval signature
Risk assessment procedure covering CIA triad
Risk treatment options documentation (accept, mitigate, transfer, avoid)
indicators_of_implementation: A documented risk management methodology exists and is formally approved
The methodology explicitly addresses confidentiality, integrity, and availability
Risk treatment options are defined within the methodology

maturity_signals.initial: Risk management is ad-hoc; no formal methodology is documented
maturity_signals.developing: A methodology document exists but lacks approval or full CIA coverage
maturity_signals.defined: Complete, approved methodology covering CIA triad, with risk treatment options defined
maturity_signals.advanced: Methodology is aligned with ISO 27005 or equivalent; risk appetite thresholds are formally set and reviewed

possible_technical_evidence: GRC platform configuration showing risk scoring methodology and CIA weightings

audit_questions: Is the risk management methodology actively implemented (not just documented)?
Is there an active risk register maintained by the cybersecurity department?
Are identified risks tracked through assessment, treatment, and monitoring?
Are risk treatment actions assigned owners and tracked to completion?
suggested_evidence: Current cybersecurity risk register with risk owners, ratings, and treatment status
Risk treatment plans with assigned owners and timelines
Risk review meeting minutes or committee reports
indicators_of_implementation: An active risk register exists and is updated regularly
Risks have treatment plans with assigned owners
Risk review cycle is defined and executed

maturity_signals.initial: Risk register exists as a one-time exercise; not maintained
maturity_signals.developing: Risk register is updated periodically; treatment plans exist for critical risks
maturity_signals.defined: Risk register is maintained continuously; all risks have owners and treatment plans are tracked
maturity_signals.advanced: Risk register is integrated with asset inventory and threat intelligence; residual risk is reported to the supervisory committee

possible_technical_evidence: GRC platform risk register export
Ticketing system showing risk treatment action tracking

audit_questions: Is there a procedure for conducting cybersecurity risk assessments at defined triggering events?
Does the procedure cover at minimum: technology project early stage, major infrastructure changes, third-party service procurement, and new technology product/service releases?
Is there evidence that risk assessments are actually conducted at each triggering event?
Are risk assessment results documented and acted upon?
suggested_evidence: Risk assessment procedure listing all mandatory triggering events
Completed risk assessment reports tied to specific triggering events
Project or change management records showing risk assessment as a gate requirement
indicators_of_implementation: Risk assessment procedure defines all mandatory triggering events from ECC-2
For each recent triggering event, a corresponding risk assessment report exists
Risk assessments are gated requirements before project or change approval

maturity_signals.initial: Risk assessments are conducted only when explicitly requested; no triggering event process exists
maturity_signals.developing: Some triggering events are covered but not all four minimum categories
maturity_signals.defined: All four minimum triggering events are covered; assessments are conducted and documented at each
maturity_signals.advanced: Risk assessment process is automated into project and change management workflows; non-compliance with gate requirements is escalated

possible_documents: Risk Assessment Procedure (with triggering events listed)
Project Management Gate Checklist including risk assessment
possible_technical_evidence: Project management system showing risk assessment completion as a mandatory gate
Change management system requiring risk assessment sign-off

audit_questions: Is a cybersecurity risk assessment mandated at the early stage of all technology projects?
Is there evidence that this assessment occurs before significant resources are committed to a project?
Are risk assessment findings incorporated into the project's security requirements?
suggested_evidence: Project initiation checklist showing early-stage risk assessment as mandatory
Risk assessment reports dated at the initiation or planning phase of technology projects
Security requirements derived from early-stage risk assessment included in project specifications
indicators_of_implementation: Project initiation governance requires a cybersecurity risk assessment before project approval
Risk assessment outputs are reflected in project security requirements

maturity_signals.initial: No formal requirement for risk assessment at project initiation
maturity_signals.developing: Risk assessment is recommended but not enforced for all projects
maturity_signals.defined: Mandatory gate for all technology projects; results documented and acted upon
maturity_signals.advanced: Early-stage assessment is integrated with project management tool; security requirements auto-generated from risk findings

possible_technical_evidence: Project management system gate records showing risk assessment completion

audit_questions: Is a cybersecurity risk assessment required before making major changes to technology infrastructure?
How is 'major change' defined and is that definition documented?
Is there evidence that risk assessments were conducted before recent infrastructure changes?
Are change management processes gated on completion of the risk assessment?
suggested_evidence: Change management policy defining 'major change' threshold
Risk assessment reports linked to specific infrastructure change requests
Change approval records showing risk assessment sign-off as a prerequisite
indicators_of_implementation: Definition of 'major change' is documented and communicated to IT and project teams
Change management workflow includes a cybersecurity risk assessment gate for major changes
Recent major changes have corresponding risk assessment reports

maturity_signals.initial: Infrastructure changes proceed without cybersecurity risk assessment
maturity_signals.developing: Risk assessment is performed for some major changes but definition is inconsistent
maturity_signals.defined: All major changes as defined are assessed; change approval is gated on completion
maturity_signals.advanced: Automated change classification triggers risk assessment workflows; residual risk is signed off by CISO

possible_technical_evidence: ITSM / change management tool records showing risk assessment approval step

audit_questions: Is a cybersecurity risk assessment required during planning for third-party service procurement?
Is the risk assessment integrated into the procurement or vendor management process?
Does the risk assessment cover data sharing, access rights, and supply chain risks?
Are third-party risk assessment results used to define contractual security requirements?
suggested_evidence: Procurement policy requiring cybersecurity risk assessment for third-party services
Third-party risk assessment reports for recent procurements
Vendor contracts with security requirements derived from risk assessment findings
indicators_of_implementation: Procurement process includes cybersecurity risk assessment as a mandatory step before vendor selection
Risk assessment outputs are reflected in contract security clauses
Third-party risk assessments are maintained in a vendor risk register

maturity_signals.initial: Third-party services are procured without cybersecurity risk consideration
maturity_signals.developing: Risk assessment is performed for some vendors but not consistently
maturity_signals.defined: Risk assessment is mandatory for all third-party service procurements; findings are reflected in contracts
maturity_signals.advanced: Third-party risk is continuously monitored post-procurement; risk ratings are updated based on security events

possible_technical_evidence: Vendor management platform records showing risk rating per vendor

audit_questions: Is a cybersecurity risk assessment required during planning and before release of new technology services and products?
Does the pre-release assessment include security testing, vulnerability assessment, and configuration review?
Is the release gated on completion and sign-off of the risk assessment?
Are risk assessment findings resolved or formally accepted before release?
suggested_evidence: Release management procedure requiring pre-release cybersecurity risk assessment
Pre-release security assessment reports for recent service/product launches
Release approval records showing cybersecurity sign-off
indicators_of_implementation: Release management process includes a cybersecurity gate before go-live
Pre-release assessments are documented and findings are tracked to resolution
Unresolved critical findings block release until resolved or formally accepted

maturity_signals.initial: Services/products are released without pre-release cybersecurity assessment
maturity_signals.developing: Security testing occurs but is not formally required; findings are not always resolved before release
maturity_signals.defined: Pre-release cybersecurity assessment is mandatory; critical findings must be resolved before release
maturity_signals.advanced: Security assessment is automated into CI/CD pipeline; risk acceptance for residual issues is documented and signed off by CISO

possible_documents: Release Approval Records with Cybersecurity Sign-Off
possible_technical_evidence: CI/CD pipeline configuration showing security gate stages
Vulnerability scan results tied to release build versions

audit_questions: Is there a defined schedule for reviewing the cybersecurity risk management methodology?
Are reviews also triggered by changes to legislative/regulatory requirements and internal standards?
Are changes to the methodology documented, approved, and version-controlled?
Is there evidence the most recent review occurred within the defined cycle?
suggested_evidence: Risk management methodology with embedded review schedule
Review log showing review dates, reviewers, and outcomes
Version-controlled methodology document with change history and approval signatures
indicators_of_implementation: Methodology review schedule is defined and documented
Most recent review falls within the defined cycle
All changes are formally approved and version-controlled

maturity_signals.initial: Methodology is never reviewed after initial creation
maturity_signals.developing: Reviews occur but are infrequent and informal
maturity_signals.defined: Reviews are on schedule and triggered by changes; all updates are formally approved
maturity_signals.advanced: Regulatory intelligence integration triggers methodology reviews; all stakeholders are notified of changes

audit_questions: Are cybersecurity requirements formally incorporated into the project management methodology?
Is cybersecurity also embedded in information and technology asset change management procedures?
Are cybersecurity requirements treated as mandatory (key) requirements rather than optional?
Is there evidence that cybersecurity risks are identified and managed as part of the technology project lifecycle?
suggested_evidence: Project management methodology document showing cybersecurity as a mandatory requirement category
Change management procedure with cybersecurity requirements section
Project templates showing cybersecurity requirements as a standard deliverable
Sample project security requirements documents from recent projects
indicators_of_implementation: Project methodology documentation explicitly includes cybersecurity requirements
Change management process requires cybersecurity review for technology asset changes
Cybersecurity is listed as a key project requirement in project initiation templates

maturity_signals.initial: Cybersecurity is considered ad-hoc in projects; no formal inclusion in project methodology
maturity_signals.developing: Cybersecurity is mentioned in project methodology but not consistently applied
maturity_signals.defined: Cybersecurity requirements are a mandatory section in all technology project and change management documentation
maturity_signals.advanced: Cybersecurity is integrated at each phase gate of the project lifecycle; CISO or delegate reviews requirements at initiation and close

possible_technical_evidence: Project management tool configuration showing cybersecurity requirements as mandatory fields

audit_questions: Do cybersecurity requirements for project management and IT asset changes include at minimum vulnerability assessment/remediation and secure configuration review?
Are these minimum requirements documented in the project and change management procedures?
Is there evidence these requirements are applied in practice for recent projects and changes?
Are the two subcontrol requirements (1-6-2-1 and 1-6-2-2) both addressed?
suggested_evidence: Project and change management procedure showing vulnerability assessment and configuration review as minimum requirements
Sample vulnerability assessment reports from recent projects and changes
Configuration review checklists completed before project launch or change go-live
indicators_of_implementation: Procedure explicitly lists vulnerability assessment/remediation and configuration/hardening review as minimum cybersecurity requirements
Both requirements are present in project and change checklists
Recent projects and changes have documented completion of both requirements

maturity_signals.initial: Neither vulnerability assessment nor configuration review is required before project launch or change
maturity_signals.developing: One of the two requirements is sometimes performed but not consistently
maturity_signals.defined: Both requirements are documented as mandatory and evidenced for all recent projects and changes
maturity_signals.advanced: Requirements are automated into SDLC/DevOps pipeline; findings are tracked in a central vulnerability management system

possible_technical_evidence: Vulnerability scanner reports tied to project/change records
Hardening assessment tool output

audit_questions: Is vulnerability assessment and remediation required for all projects and infrastructure changes?
Are vulnerability assessments documented and tracked to remediation?
Are critical or high vulnerabilities resolved before project launch or change go-live?
Is there a defined SLA for remediating identified vulnerabilities by severity?
suggested_evidence: Vulnerability assessment reports per project/change with severity ratings
Remediation tracking records showing vulnerabilities resolved before launch/go-live
Vulnerability management SLA policy or procedure
indicators_of_implementation: Vulnerability assessments are conducted as part of every project and major change
Findings are tracked in a vulnerability management system or register
Launch/go-live gates require all critical/high findings to be remediated or risk-accepted

maturity_signals.initial: Vulnerability assessments are not conducted before launch or change
maturity_signals.developing: Assessments are conducted but remediation is not tracked to closure
maturity_signals.defined: Assessments are mandatory; critical/high findings must be resolved before launch; tracked in a register
maturity_signals.advanced: Automated scanning integrated into CI/CD; vulnerability trends are reported to governance; SLA breach alerts are sent

possible_technical_evidence: Vulnerability scanner output (e.g., Tenable, Qualys, Rapid7)
Ticketing system records showing vulnerability remediation workflow

audit_questions: Is secure configuration and hardening reviewed before launching projects and changes?
Are software/firmware updates (patches) verified and applied before go-live?
Is there a hardening baseline defined for each technology platform?
Are deviations from the hardening baseline documented and approved?
suggested_evidence: Hardening checklists completed before project launch or change go-live
Patch management records showing updates applied before launch
Configuration review reports with sign-off by cybersecurity team
indicators_of_implementation: Hardening baselines exist for all relevant platforms
Pre-launch/pre-go-live checklist includes configuration and hardening review
Patch levels are verified and documented before each launch or change

maturity_signals.initial: No hardening or patch verification before launch/go-live
maturity_signals.developing: Some platforms are hardened before launch; not consistently applied
maturity_signals.defined: Hardening and patch verification is mandatory for all platforms before launch/change; evidence is documented
maturity_signals.advanced: Automated compliance checks against CIS benchmarks or equivalent; non-compliant configurations block deployment

possible_technical_evidence: Configuration compliance scan output (e.g., CIS-CAT)
Patch management system showing patch status at time of launch

audit_questions: Do cybersecurity requirements for software and application development include at minimum the five requirements listed in subcontrols 1-6-3-1 through 1-6-3-5?
Are these requirements documented in a secure software development lifecycle (SSDLC) policy or procedure?
Is there evidence these requirements are applied in practice in development projects?
Do development teams receive training on secure development requirements?
suggested_evidence: SSDLC policy or secure coding standard covering all five minimum requirements
Code review records, security testing reports, and compliance test results from recent development projects
Training records for developers on secure coding and SSDLC requirements
indicators_of_implementation: All five minimum requirements are explicitly listed in the development security procedure
Development projects produce evidence of compliance with each requirement
Development team has completed training on the SSDLC requirements

maturity_signals.initial: No formal secure development requirements; security is addressed ad-hoc
maturity_signals.developing: Some requirements are followed but coverage is incomplete; not all five minimums are addressed
maturity_signals.defined: All five requirements are documented, mandatory, and evidenced for all development projects
maturity_signals.advanced: SSDLC is integrated with DevSecOps pipeline; automated security gates enforce requirements at each stage

possible_technical_evidence: SAST/DAST scan results from CI/CD pipeline
Software Composition Analysis (SCA) reports

audit_questions: Is the use of secure coding standards mandatory for all software development within the entity?
Which secure coding standards are adopted (e.g., OWASP, CERT, SANS)?
Are developers trained on the adopted standards?
Are code reviews conducted to verify adherence to secure coding standards?
suggested_evidence: Adopted secure coding standard document (e.g., OWASP Secure Coding Practices)
Code review records showing security-focused review steps
Developer training completion records for secure coding
indicators_of_implementation: A specific secure coding standard is formally adopted and referenced in the SSDLC policy
Code reviews include security checks against the standard
Developers have completed training on the adopted standard

maturity_signals.initial: No secure coding standard is defined or communicated to developers
maturity_signals.developing: A standard is referenced but not consistently applied; code reviews do not systematically check security
maturity_signals.defined: Standard is adopted, developers are trained, and code reviews verify adherence
maturity_signals.advanced: SAST tools automatically enforce coding standards; violations block CI/CD pipeline merges

possible_technical_evidence: SAST tool reports (e.g., SonarQube, Checkmarx)
CI/CD security gate configuration

audit_questions: Is there a policy requiring the use of trusted and licensed sources for software development tools and libraries?
Is there a process to vet and approve third-party libraries before use?
Is a software bill of materials (SBOM) or equivalent maintained for development projects?
Are open-source libraries checked for known vulnerabilities before use?
suggested_evidence: Software supply chain or third-party library approval policy
Approved software and library register
License compliance records for all development tools
SCA scan reports showing vetted libraries
indicators_of_implementation: Policy requires use of trusted and licensed sources only
An approval process exists before new libraries or tools are used
All current development tools and libraries are documented with license and source information

maturity_signals.initial: Developers use any available library without vetting or approval
maturity_signals.developing: Some libraries are reviewed but process is informal; license compliance is not tracked
maturity_signals.defined: Formal approval process for libraries; license compliance tracked; known vulnerability scanning performed
maturity_signals.advanced: Automated SCA in CI/CD pipeline blocks use of libraries with critical vulnerabilities or unapproved licenses

possible_technical_evidence: SCA tool output (e.g., OWASP Dependency-Check, Snyk, Black Duck)
Package manager lock files with version pins

audit_questions: Is compliance testing of software against internal cybersecurity requirements conducted before release?
Are the cybersecurity requirements that must be tested formally documented?
Are compliance test results documented and reviewed before release approval?
Are non-compliant findings resolved before release or formally accepted?
suggested_evidence: Pre-release security compliance test checklist or test cases mapped to cybersecurity requirements
Compliance test results reports for recent software releases
Release gate records showing cybersecurity compliance sign-off
indicators_of_implementation: A compliance test suite exists that maps to the entity's cybersecurity requirements
Compliance tests are run for every software release
Failures are tracked and must be resolved before release approval

maturity_signals.initial: No compliance testing against cybersecurity requirements before release
maturity_signals.developing: Some security testing occurs but not mapped to internal cybersecurity requirements
maturity_signals.defined: Compliance test cases are mapped to requirements; results are documented and gate releases
maturity_signals.advanced: Automated compliance testing integrated into CI/CD; results are linked to the risk register; trends are reported to governance

possible_technical_evidence: Automated test results from security test suites in CI/CD
DAST scan results (e.g., OWASP ZAP, Burp Suite)

audit_questions: Are security requirements for integration between applications formally defined?
Is API and application integration security reviewed as part of the development and change process?
Are integration points tested for security vulnerabilities (e.g., authentication, authorization, data exposure)?
Are integration security standards documented and communicated to development teams?
suggested_evidence: Integration security standards or API security guidelines
Integration security review records from development projects
API security test results (authentication, authorization, input validation)
indicators_of_implementation: Integration security requirements are documented and part of the SSDLC
All application integrations are security-reviewed before go-live
API and integration testing includes security test cases

maturity_signals.initial: No security requirements for application integration; integrations go live without security review
maturity_signals.developing: Some integration security checks exist but are informal
maturity_signals.defined: Integration security standards are defined; all integrations are reviewed and tested against standards before go-live
maturity_signals.advanced: API security testing automated in CI/CD; integration security posture monitored continuously in production

possible_technical_evidence: API security scanner results (e.g., 42Crunch, Noname Security)
Integration test reports showing security test cases

audit_questions: Is a secure configuration, hardening, and patch review conducted before launching software products?
Are deployment configurations reviewed against defined security baselines?
Are all software dependencies and the runtime environment patched before launch?
Are deviations from the secure configuration baseline documented and approved before launch?
suggested_evidence: Pre-launch secure configuration checklist for software products
Hardening review reports for deployment environments
Patch status records at time of software product launch
indicators_of_implementation: A pre-launch checklist covers secure configuration and hardening for all software products
Deployment configurations are verified against baselines before launch
All patches and updates are applied before launch or deviations are formally documented

maturity_signals.initial: Software products launch without secure configuration review
maturity_signals.developing: Configuration is reviewed informally; not all components are covered
maturity_signals.defined: Mandatory pre-launch configuration and patch review for all software products; evidence is documented
maturity_signals.advanced: Infrastructure-as-code security scanning (e.g., Checkov, tfsec) and automated patch verification gate software releases

possible_technical_evidence: IaC security scanner output
Container image scan results
Patch management system showing patch status at launch date

audit_questions: Is there a defined schedule for periodically reviewing cybersecurity requirements for project management?
Are reviews triggered by changes in technology, regulatory requirements, or security landscape?
Are changes to project management cybersecurity requirements documented and approved?
Is there evidence the most recent review occurred within the defined cycle?
suggested_evidence: Project management cybersecurity requirements review log
Revised project management procedures with change history
Approval records for updated requirements
indicators_of_implementation: A defined review schedule exists for project management cybersecurity requirements
Most recent review falls within the cycle
Updates are formally approved and communicated to project teams

maturity_signals.initial: Project management security requirements are never reviewed after initial creation
maturity_signals.developing: Reviews occur informally and infrequently
maturity_signals.defined: Reviews are on a defined schedule; changes are approved and communicated
maturity_signals.advanced: Review process is linked to regulatory intelligence; new threat patterns trigger ad-hoc reviews

possible_documents: Project Management Security Requirements Review Log

audit_questions: Has the entity identified all nationally approved international agreements or commitments containing cybersecurity requirements applicable to it?
Is there a register of applicable international cybersecurity obligations?
Is there evidence that the entity complies with the cybersecurity requirements in these agreements?
Is the register kept current as new agreements are adopted nationally?
suggested_evidence: Register of applicable nationally approved international agreements with cybersecurity requirements
Compliance assessment report mapping agreement requirements to entity controls
Legal or regulatory tracking mechanism for new internationally adopted agreements
indicators_of_implementation: Entity has conducted a scoping exercise to identify applicable international agreements
Each identified agreement's cybersecurity requirements are mapped to entity controls
Compliance status against each agreement is documented and monitored

maturity_signals.initial: No assessment has been conducted to identify applicable international agreements
maturity_signals.developing: Some agreements have been identified but compliance mapping is incomplete
maturity_signals.defined: All applicable agreements are identified; compliance requirements are mapped and monitored
maturity_signals.advanced: Regulatory intelligence capability monitors for new nationally approved agreements; compliance gap analysis is automated

possible_documents: Compliance Mapping: International Agreements to Entity Controls

audit_questions: Does the cybersecurity department conduct periodic self-reviews of control implementation?
Is there a defined frequency for these reviews?
Are review results documented and acted upon?
Are review findings reported to appropriate governance bodies?
suggested_evidence: Internal cybersecurity control review reports with dates and scope
Review schedule or calendar showing periodic review cadence
Corrective action plans resulting from review findings
indicators_of_implementation: A defined review frequency is documented
Reviews are conducted on schedule and results are documented
Findings from reviews result in corrective actions that are tracked to closure

maturity_signals.initial: No periodic internal review of cybersecurity controls
maturity_signals.developing: Reviews occur but are infrequent or inconsistent; findings are not systematically tracked
maturity_signals.defined: Periodic reviews conducted on schedule; findings are documented and corrective actions tracked
maturity_signals.advanced: Review findings are fed into the risk register and supervisory committee; trending analysis used to prioritize improvements

possible_technical_evidence: GRC platform dashboard showing control review status and findings

audit_questions: Are cybersecurity controls audited by parties independent from the cybersecurity department?
Is the independence of auditors documented (no conflict of interest)?
Are audits conducted in accordance with Generally Accepted Auditing Standards (GAAS)?
Is there evidence of the most recent independent audit and its scope?
suggested_evidence: Independent audit reports from internal audit, external auditors, or accredited third parties
Auditor independence declaration or engagement letter confirming no conflict of interest
Audit methodology documentation referencing GAAS compliance
indicators_of_implementation: Independent audits are conducted at a defined frequency
Auditors are demonstrably independent from the cybersecurity department
Audit methodology references GAAS or equivalent standards

maturity_signals.initial: Cybersecurity is only reviewed internally by the cybersecurity department; no independent audit
maturity_signals.developing: Some independent review occurs but auditor independence is not formally documented
maturity_signals.defined: Independent audits are conducted periodically; independence is documented; GAAS compliance is referenced
maturity_signals.advanced: Audit program includes both internal independent audits and external third-party assessments; results are benchmarked year-over-year

audit_questions: Are audit and review results formally documented?
Are results presented to both the cybersecurity supervisory committee and the Authorized Official?
Do the results include: scope, observations, recommendations, corrective actions, and remediation plans?
Are remediation plans tracked to completion after presentation?
suggested_evidence: Audit report and executive summary presented to supervisory committee and Authorized Official
Supervisory committee and Authorized Official meeting minutes showing receipt and discussion of audit results
Remediation plan tracker showing progress against each audit finding
indicators_of_implementation: Audit reports include all five required elements: scope, observations, recommendations, corrective actions, remediation plans
Evidence of formal presentation to supervisory committee and Authorized Official
Remediation plans are tracked and status-reported at subsequent governance meetings

maturity_signals.initial: Audit results are shared informally; not presented to supervisory committee or Authorized Official
maturity_signals.developing: Results are presented to committee but remediation tracking is informal
maturity_signals.defined: Results include all required elements and are presented to both required parties; remediation is tracked
maturity_signals.advanced: Audit findings are integrated into the risk register; remediation SLAs are defined and monitored; trends are reported each cycle

possible_technical_evidence: GRC platform showing audit finding status and remediation progress

audit_questions: Are cybersecurity requirements for personnel formally identified and documented covering all three employment phases: prior to, during, and upon end/termination of employment?
Is the document approved by the Authorized Official?
Are all three phases explicitly addressed in the documentation?
Are the requirements communicated to HR and relevant managers?
suggested_evidence: Cybersecurity HR requirements policy or procedure covering all three employment phases
Approval record for the policy
Communication records showing the policy was shared with HR and management
indicators_of_implementation: A documented policy exists covering pre-employment, during employment, and termination phases
Policy is approved by the Authorized Official
HR procedures align with and reference the cybersecurity HR requirements

maturity_signals.initial: No formal cybersecurity requirements for personnel lifecycle; requirements are informal
maturity_signals.developing: Some phases are covered but documentation is incomplete
maturity_signals.defined: All three phases are documented and approved; HR procedures are aligned
maturity_signals.advanced: Cybersecurity HR requirements are integrated into automated HR workflows; compliance is monitored

possible_technical_evidence: HR system configuration showing cybersecurity-required process steps for onboarding and offboarding

audit_questions: Is there evidence that cybersecurity HR requirements are actually implemented, not just documented?
Are pre-employment, during-employment, and termination processes consistently following cybersecurity requirements?
Are exceptions or non-compliance tracked and addressed?
Are compliance checks embedded into HR processes (onboarding/offboarding checklists)?
suggested_evidence: Completed onboarding and offboarding checklists showing cybersecurity requirement steps
HR audit or compliance review results for cybersecurity requirements
Non-compliance records showing corrective actions taken
indicators_of_implementation: HR checklists include cybersecurity requirement steps for all three phases
Recent onboarding and offboarding records show completion of cybersecurity steps
Non-compliance cases are documented and resolved

maturity_signals.initial: Requirements are documented but implementation is inconsistent
maturity_signals.developing: Most HR processes include cybersecurity steps; some gaps remain
maturity_signals.defined: Cybersecurity requirements are consistently embedded in all HR processes; compliance is monitored
maturity_signals.advanced: Automated HR workflow enforces cybersecurity requirements; non-compliance triggers immediate alerts

possible_technical_evidence: HR system workflow records showing completion of cybersecurity steps
Access management system showing timely access revocation on termination

audit_questions: Are pre-employment cybersecurity requirements formally defined and implemented?
Do they include at minimum: cybersecurity clauses in employment contracts (1-9-3-1) and screening for cybersecurity/critical/privileged positions (1-9-3-2)?
Is there evidence that employment contracts for all new hires include the required cybersecurity clauses?
Is there evidence that screening is conducted for qualifying positions before hire?
suggested_evidence: Standard employment contract template showing cybersecurity responsibility and NDA clauses
Screening procedure for cybersecurity and critical/privileged positions
Screening records for recent hires in qualifying positions
indicators_of_implementation: All employment contracts include cybersecurity responsibility and non-disclosure clauses
Screening is defined and conducted for cybersecurity and critical/privileged roles
Pre-employment process checklist confirms both requirements are met before hiring

maturity_signals.initial: No cybersecurity clauses in contracts; no screening for any positions
maturity_signals.developing: Clauses exist in some contracts; screening is applied inconsistently
maturity_signals.defined: All contracts include required clauses; screening is mandatory and evidenced for qualifying positions
maturity_signals.advanced: Clauses cover both during and post-employment obligations; screening includes background checks by a qualified provider; records retained per retention policy

possible_technical_evidence: HR system records confirming contract clause completion and screening status

audit_questions: Do all employment contracts include cybersecurity responsibilities clauses?
Do all contracts include non-disclosure clauses covering both during and after employment?
Are these clauses present in contracts for all personnel (employees and contractors)?
Is there a process to ensure new contract templates always include these clauses?
suggested_evidence: Current employment contract template showing cybersecurity and NDA clauses
Sample signed contracts from recent hires confirming clause inclusion
Contractor agreement template with equivalent clauses
indicators_of_implementation: Standard contract templates include both cybersecurity responsibilities and NDA clauses
Clauses cover obligations during and after employment ends
Contractor agreements contain equivalent provisions

maturity_signals.initial: No cybersecurity or NDA clauses in employment contracts
maturity_signals.developing: Clauses exist for employees but not contractors, or post-employment obligations are missing
maturity_signals.defined: All contracts (employees and contractors) include both types of clauses covering during and post-employment
maturity_signals.advanced: Legal reviews cybersecurity clauses periodically to ensure adequacy; clause changes are tracked with version history

possible_technical_evidence: HR system contract completion records

audit_questions: Is pre-employment screening conducted for all cybersecurity positions?
Is screening also conducted for technical positions with critical and privileged powers?
What does screening cover (identity verification, criminal record, employment history)?
Are screening records maintained and is the process documented?
suggested_evidence: Pre-employment screening policy defining scope (which roles) and depth of screening
Screening records or certificates for recent hires in qualifying positions
Third-party screening provider engagement records (if applicable)
indicators_of_implementation: Screening policy defines which positions are in scope
Screening is consistently conducted before employment commences for in-scope roles
Screening records are retained per the defined retention period

maturity_signals.initial: No screening for any positions before hire
maturity_signals.developing: Screening for some roles; scope and depth are inconsistent
maturity_signals.defined: Systematic screening for all cybersecurity and critical/privileged positions; records maintained
maturity_signals.advanced: Periodic rescreening for high-risk roles; screening criteria reviewed annually and updated to reflect risk profile

possible_technical_evidence: HR system showing screening status for all in-scope hires

audit_questions: Are cybersecurity requirements for personnel during employment formally defined and implemented?
Do they include at minimum: cybersecurity awareness (1-9-4-1) and implementation of and compliance with cybersecurity requirements (1-9-4-2)?
Is there evidence both requirements are embedded in during-employment processes?
Are managers accountable for employee compliance with cybersecurity requirements?
suggested_evidence: During-employment cybersecurity requirements policy
Cybersecurity awareness program records (onboarding and ongoing)
Compliance monitoring records for during-employment cybersecurity obligations
indicators_of_implementation: Policy defines cybersecurity requirements for all employees during employment
Both subcontrol requirements (awareness and compliance) are evidenced
Manager accountability for employee compliance is documented

maturity_signals.initial: No formal during-employment cybersecurity requirements; ad-hoc awareness only
maturity_signals.developing: Some requirements defined but compliance monitoring is absent
maturity_signals.defined: Both requirements defined and implemented; compliance is monitored
maturity_signals.advanced: Automated compliance monitoring; non-compliance triggers HR escalation; awareness effectiveness is measured

possible_technical_evidence: LMS training records
Access management logs showing compliance with access controls

audit_questions: Is cybersecurity awareness training provided during onboarding for all new employees?
Is ongoing cybersecurity awareness training provided during employment?
Are training completion records maintained?
Is training effectiveness measured (e.g., through assessments or phishing simulations)?
suggested_evidence: Onboarding training curriculum showing cybersecurity awareness module
Ongoing training records (annual or periodic) for all employees
Training completion reports from LMS or HR system
Training effectiveness assessment results
indicators_of_implementation: All new employees receive cybersecurity awareness training at onboarding
All employees receive periodic refresher training
Training completion is tracked and reported

maturity_signals.initial: No formal cybersecurity awareness training; only ad-hoc communication
maturity_signals.developing: Training exists at onboarding but no ongoing refresh; completion not tracked
maturity_signals.defined: Onboarding and periodic training; completion tracked and non-completion followed up
maturity_signals.advanced: Training program is risk-based; effectiveness measured via phishing simulations and knowledge assessments; low-performers receive additional training

possible_technical_evidence: LMS export showing completion per employee
Phishing simulation results

audit_questions: Are employees required to comply with all cybersecurity policies, procedures, and operational requirements?
Is this obligation documented and communicated to all employees?
Is non-compliance tracked and addressed through HR or disciplinary processes?
Are employees periodically reminded of their compliance obligations?
suggested_evidence: Employee acknowledgment records for cybersecurity policies
Non-compliance register showing disciplinary actions taken
HR procedure for handling cybersecurity policy violations
indicators_of_implementation: All employees have acknowledged their compliance obligations
Non-compliance cases are handled through a defined HR process
Compliance obligations are reinforced through periodic communication

maturity_signals.initial: Compliance obligation is not formally communicated or acknowledged
maturity_signals.developing: Policy acknowledgment exists but non-compliance process is informal
maturity_signals.defined: All employees acknowledge obligations; non-compliance is addressed through HR; records are maintained
maturity_signals.advanced: Compliance is monitored through technical controls; repeated non-compliance triggers escalation; accountability is included in performance reviews

possible_technical_evidence: DLP or UEBA alert records showing compliance monitoring

audit_questions: Is there a process to immediately review and revoke personnel powers upon end/termination of employment?
Does 'immediately' have a defined SLA (e.g., same day, within 24 hours)?
Are access revocations tracked and confirmed for all systems and physical access?
Is there evidence of prompt access revocation for recent terminations?
suggested_evidence: Offboarding procedure with defined SLA for access revocation
Access revocation records (logical and physical) for recent terminations
HR-to-IT notification process records (e.g., termination tickets)
Periodic access review confirming no terminated users retain access
indicators_of_implementation: A defined SLA for access revocation exists and is communicated to HR and IT
Access revocations are tracked across all systems (including cloud, VPN, email, physical)
Recent termination records show access was revoked within the defined SLA

maturity_signals.initial: Access revocation is handled manually and informally; timing is inconsistent
maturity_signals.developing: Process exists but SLA is not defined or consistently met; some access may remain after termination
maturity_signals.defined: Defined SLA for access revocation; process covers all systems; compliance is spot-checked
maturity_signals.advanced: Automated offboarding workflow triggers immediate access revocation across all systems; compliance is monitored and exceptions are auto-escalated

possible_technical_evidence: IAM system logs showing access revocation timestamps
AD / Entra ID account disable logs correlated with termination dates
Physical access control system deactivation records

audit_questions: Is there a defined review schedule for cybersecurity HR requirements?
Is the review triggered by HR policy changes, regulatory updates, or significant incidents?
Are updates to HR requirements formally approved and communicated to HR and personnel?
Is there evidence of the most recent review?
suggested_evidence: HR requirements review log showing review dates and outcomes
Version history of the cybersecurity HR requirements policy
Communication records notifying HR of updated requirements
indicators_of_implementation: A review schedule is defined for cybersecurity HR requirements
Most recent review falls within the defined cycle
Updates are approved and HR is notified

maturity_signals.initial: HR requirements are never reviewed after initial creation
maturity_signals.developing: Occasional reviews occur but with no defined schedule
maturity_signals.defined: Reviews on a defined schedule; updates approved and communicated to HR
maturity_signals.advanced: Review process includes lessons learned from HR-related security incidents; updates are reflected in onboarding/offboarding processes promptly

audit_questions: Is there a formally developed and approved cybersecurity awareness program?
Is the program delivered through multiple channels?
Is the program periodically developed (not a one-time creation)?
Does the program aim to strengthen awareness culture, not just disseminate information?
Is the program approved by the Authorized Official?
suggested_evidence: Cybersecurity awareness program document with approval signature
Program content covering multiple delivery channels (e-mail, intranet, posters, LMS, workshops)
Annual program plan or calendar
Evidence of program execution (delivery records, attendance, completion)
indicators_of_implementation: Program is formally approved and documented
Multiple channels are used for delivery
Program is renewed/refreshed on a defined cycle rather than a one-time effort
Program reaches all personnel

maturity_signals.initial: No formal awareness program; ad-hoc emails only
maturity_signals.developing: A program exists but is delivered through a single channel; not refreshed regularly
maturity_signals.defined: Multi-channel program; periodically refreshed; approved by Authorized Official
maturity_signals.advanced: Program is tailored by role and risk profile; effectiveness is measured; results inform the next cycle

possible_technical_evidence: LMS program enrollment and completion records
Email campaign analytics

audit_questions: Is the approved awareness program actually implemented (not just planned)?
Is there evidence of program delivery across the entity?
Are all employees reached by the program?
Is implementation tracked and non-participation followed up?
suggested_evidence: Delivery records showing program was executed as planned
LMS completion records or attendance registers
Follow-up records for non-participating employees
indicators_of_implementation: Program is implemented as per the approved plan
Delivery is evidenced for all channels
Completion rates are tracked and reported

maturity_signals.initial: Program is approved but not implemented
maturity_signals.developing: Program is partially implemented; coverage of all staff is not tracked
maturity_signals.defined: Full implementation evidenced; completion tracked; non-participation is followed up
maturity_signals.advanced: Completion metrics are reported to governance; low-completion departments receive targeted interventions

possible_technical_evidence: LMS system completion report by department
Email campaign delivery and open rate reports

audit_questions: Does the awareness program include content on protecting against the most important and latest cyber risks and threats?
Does the program cover all four minimum topics in subcontrols 1-10-3-1 to 1-10-3-4?
Is program content updated to reflect the latest threats?
Is there evidence all four topics are covered in the most recent program cycle?
suggested_evidence: Awareness program content index showing all four required topics
Training materials for each of the four topics
Content update records showing topics are refreshed with current threat information
indicators_of_implementation: Program content explicitly covers all four minimum topics
Content is updated to reflect current threat landscape (not static material from years ago)
Topic coverage is evidenced in training completion records

maturity_signals.initial: Awareness program does not cover the four mandatory topics
maturity_signals.developing: Some topics are covered; not all four minimum topics are addressed
maturity_signals.defined: All four topics covered; content is updated annually
maturity_signals.advanced: Content is threat-intelligence-driven; topics are updated in near-real-time with emerging threats; role-specific modules exist

possible_technical_evidence: LMS course catalog showing all four topic modules
Phishing simulation results correlated with phishing awareness training

audit_questions: Does the awareness program include training on secure handling of email services?
Does training specifically address phishing email identification and reporting?
Is phishing simulation conducted to test employee awareness?
Are employees who fail phishing simulations given remedial training?
suggested_evidence: Email security and phishing awareness training module
Phishing simulation campaign records showing frequency and results
Remedial training records for employees who failed simulations
indicators_of_implementation: Email security and anti-phishing content is included in the awareness program
Phishing simulations are conducted periodically
Failure rates and remediation are tracked

maturity_signals.initial: No email security or phishing awareness training
maturity_signals.developing: Training exists but no phishing simulations; effectiveness not measured
maturity_signals.defined: Training and simulations conducted; failure rates tracked; remediation in place
maturity_signals.advanced: Phishing simulation frequency and difficulty adapted based on risk; click rates trend downward over time; results reported to governance

possible_technical_evidence: Phishing simulation platform reports (e.g., KnowBe4, Proofpoint)
LMS module completion for email security training

audit_questions: Does the awareness program include training on secure handling of mobile devices?
Does training cover secure handling of storage media (USB drives, external hard drives)?
Are policies on mobile device and storage media use communicated as part of awareness training?
Are employees aware of the risks associated with lost/stolen devices and uncontrolled media?
suggested_evidence: Mobile device and storage media security training module
Mobile device policy and storage media policy communicated to all staff
Training records confirming employee completion of this module
indicators_of_implementation: Specific training content exists on mobile device and storage media security
Employees are aware of the policies governing device and media use
Training completion is tracked

maturity_signals.initial: No training on mobile device or storage media security
maturity_signals.developing: Policy exists but is not communicated through awareness training
maturity_signals.defined: Training module covers both topics; completion tracked
maturity_signals.advanced: Training includes scenario-based exercises; aligned with MDM and DLP technical controls

possible_documents: Mobile Device & Storage Media Security Training Module
possible_technical_evidence: LMS completion records for this module

audit_questions: Does the awareness program include training on secure Internet browsing?
Does training cover risks such as malicious websites, drive-by downloads, and unsecured Wi-Fi?
Are employees aware of the entity's acceptable use policy for Internet access?
Is training reinforced with technical controls (e.g., web filtering)?
suggested_evidence: Secure Internet browsing training module
Acceptable use policy communicating Internet usage rules
Training completion records
indicators_of_implementation: Training content covers secure Internet browsing risks and practices
Acceptable use policy is referenced in or supported by the training
Training completion is tracked

maturity_signals.initial: No Internet security awareness training
maturity_signals.developing: Generic Internet safety content included; not updated with current threats
maturity_signals.defined: Dedicated module covering current threats; tied to acceptable use policy
maturity_signals.advanced: Browsing security training aligned with web proxy/filtering configurations; real incidents used as case studies

possible_technical_evidence: LMS completion records for this module
Web filtering policy configuration

audit_questions: Does the awareness program include training on secure usage of social media?
Does training cover risks of information disclosure and social engineering via social media?
Is there a social media usage policy and is it communicated through training?
Are employees aware of which entity information must not be shared on social media?
suggested_evidence: Social media security training module
Social media usage policy
Training records confirming employee completion
indicators_of_implementation: Training includes social media security risks relevant to the organization
Social media policy is communicated and acknowledged
Training completion is tracked

maturity_signals.initial: No social media security awareness
maturity_signals.developing: Policy exists but not included in awareness training
maturity_signals.defined: Dedicated training module on social media security; policy acknowledgment tracked
maturity_signals.advanced: Training includes current social engineering case studies; policy is reviewed annually with legal and HR

possible_technical_evidence: LMS completion records for this module

audit_questions: Are specialized cybersecurity skills and training provided to personnel in positions linked directly to cybersecurity?
Are training requirements classified by cybersecurity responsibilities (covering subcontrols 1-10-4-1, 1-10-4-2, and 1-10-4-3)?
Is there a training needs analysis for cybersecurity-linked positions?
Is training completion tracked separately for cybersecurity-linked roles?
suggested_evidence: Training needs analysis for cybersecurity, development, and executive/supervisory positions
Specialized training plan per role category
Training completion records by role category
Certification or skill verification records
indicators_of_implementation: Training requirements are classified by the three role categories
Each category has a defined training curriculum or minimum training requirements
Completion is tracked and gaps are acted upon

maturity_signals.initial: No specialized training for cybersecurity-linked positions beyond general awareness
maturity_signals.developing: Some specialized training exists for cybersecurity department staff only
maturity_signals.defined: Training requirements defined and tracked for all three role categories
maturity_signals.advanced: Competency framework per role; training is tied to professional development plans; certifications are tracked and renewed

possible_technical_evidence: LMS training records filtered by role category

audit_questions: Do cybersecurity department personnel receive specialized cybersecurity skills training beyond general awareness?
Are training requirements defined for each cybersecurity role within the department?
Is there a budget and plan for ongoing professional development for cybersecurity staff?
Are professional certifications tracked and supported for cybersecurity personnel?
suggested_evidence: Training plans for each cybersecurity department role
Training completion records for cybersecurity department personnel
Certification tracking register (e.g., CISSP, CISM, CEH, CompTIA Security+)
Professional development budget records
indicators_of_implementation: Each cybersecurity role has defined training and skill requirements
Training completion is tracked per individual
Professional development is actively supported and budgeted

maturity_signals.developing: Some specialized training exists but is not planned or tracked systematically
maturity_signals.defined: Training plans exist per role; completion tracked; certifications supported
maturity_signals.advanced: Competency matrix per role; skills gaps identified annually; training linked to performance reviews and succession planning

Training platform enrollment records

audit_questions: Do software/application developers and IT asset staff receive specialized cybersecurity skills training?
Does training cover secure development practices, secure coding, and security testing?
Are training requirements defined for development and IT asset roles?

Secure coding and application security training records
Training completion records for development and IT teams
indicators_of_implementation: Developers and IT asset staff have defined cybersecurity training requirements
Training covers secure development, not just general awareness
Completion is tracked per individual

audit_questions: Do executive and supervisory positions receive specialized cybersecurity training appropriate to their responsibilities?
Does training cover cybersecurity risk management, governance, and decision-making?

Training completion records for executive and supervisory roles
Executive briefing records on cybersecurity (e.g., board presentations, CISO briefings)
indicators_of_implementation: Executives and supervisors have defined cybersecurity training requirements relevant to their roles
Training covers governance and risk management (not just technical topics)
Training completion is tracked

maturity_signals.initial: No specialized cybersecurity training for executives or supervisors
maturity_signals.developing: General awareness training only; not tailored to governance responsibilities
maturity_signals.defined: Executive and supervisory training plan exists; governance-focused content; completion tracked

audit_questions: Is there a defined schedule for periodically reviewing the cybersecurity awareness program implementation?
Does the review assess effectiveness of the program (not just delivery)?
Are review findings used to improve the program for the next cycle?
Is there evidence of the most recent review?
suggested_evidence: Awareness program review report with effectiveness metrics
Evidence of program adjustments made based on review findings
Survey results, completion rates, phishing simulation trends, or other effectiveness indicators
indicators_of_implementation: A review cycle is defined for the awareness program
Review assesses effectiveness using measurable indicators
Program is improved based on review outcomes

maturity_signals.initial: Program is never reviewed; no effectiveness measurement

maturity_signals.defined: Periodic review assesses both delivery and effectiveness; findings drive program updates
